intent:
PREMISE CONTRACT (story 9)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator phones their absent friend and discovers the friend is well from the brightness of their voice.
  change_of_state:       at s0 the friend is missing from school and the narrator does not know their state; at closer the narrator has heard the friend's bright lively voice and knows. The discovery transfers across the call. X != Y.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a park_bench / rainy_doorway / shop_counter story. This is a phone_call (seed plan slot 9 of 10) — first call scene in the corpus.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is the friend's 声 (voice) carried by the 電話 (phone). Neither has appeared before; phone is the medium, voice is the discovered object — the closer lands on the voice, not on the phone-call act.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: contrastive bittersweet — s5 'その元気な声は明るいです。' lands on what was discovered (voice's brightness); s6 'でも、友達は学校にいません。' lands on what HASN'T changed (friend still absent). でも-clause introduces N5_demo grammar and gives the closer narrative wrinkle (mirrors story 7's 'タオルを取りますが…濡れています' contrastive shape only in spirit, not in token-pattern). Not a noun-pile, not 'NのN だね' (story 6), not 'だから…見ています' (story 8).
  why_not_filler:        narrator earns slot as the asker / discoverer; friend earns slot as the absent voice whose state IS the discovery; phone earns slot as the only medium for cross-distance discovery (in-person is impossible per seed plan); 元気 is doubly load-bearing (answers the question at s3 AND anchors the new na-adjective grammar at s4-s5); 声 carries the closer.
  obligations_absorbed:  must-reinforce G037_ka_question (organic question s2 '元気ですか。'), G054_yo_emphasis (organic answer s3 '元気ですよ。'); must-introduce — brief shows must_introduce=False, but seed plan slot 9 prescribes one new grammar point. Introducing N5_na_adj via '元気な声' at s4 (and reused implicitly at s5 via そ-anaphor). Seed plan also prescribed N5_to_quote and N5_itsu_when; quotative-と is absorbed by existing G010_to_and tagger so cannot be 'introduced' here, and いつ is deferred (would force 来る/行く mints that overflow the 5-mint budget — documented deferral, no override consumed). Tense: present-tense narration throughout, including s0 ('いません') — JP narrative present is natural for a same-day frame and avoids the past/present mismatch that an earlier draft introduced.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3 stories): park_bench, rainy_doorway, shop_counter
  anchor_object  (last 5 stories): paper, pencil, towel, umbrella, 本
  closer_shape   (last 3 stories): P:だから-noun-noun-WO-noun-X:います, noun-NO-noun-DESU-P:ね, noun-noun-WO-noun-GA-noun-WA-noun-X:います
  opening_seqs   (last 3 stories' first 3 content tokens): 公園/椅子/母, 降る/雨/冷たい, 私/本屋/います

Voice: a small concrete exchange — absence answered by voice. No pathetic-fallacy on the phone itself. Subject pronoun 私は elided in s1 and s2 per JP narrative idiom (textbook-stiff '私は…します' rejected by the §E.7.5 native-naturalness pass). Mints align exactly with seed plan vocab (電話, 友達 already exists, 声, 話す, 今日) plus 元気 to anchor the new na-adjective grammar.

sentences.jp: 今日、友達は学校にいません。
sentences.en: Today my friend is not at school.

sentences.jp: だから電話で友達と話します。
sentences.en: So I talk with my friend by phone.

sentences.jp: 「元気ですよ。」と友達は答えます。
電話で、友達の元気な声を聞きます。
sentences.en: Through the phone, I hear my friend's lively voice.

sentences.jp: その元気な声は明るいです。
でも、友達は学校にいません。
sentences.en: But my friend is not at school.
